summary:
Child mortality typically considers children from birth to just after their fifth birthday; however, since the context emphasizes high-risk periods immediately post-birth through infancy, a narrower definition including newborns makes sense within these specific parameters. Age limit set by options provided likely focuses closely around initial life-threatening phases experienced primarily during first months or year(s). To be precise per requirements while acknowledging nuances discussed earlier,
The primary concern lies between **newborn** stage till approximately one-year-old as critical risk window impacting overall infant/early-childhood mortality statistics significantly more than later preschool years do individually assessed herein. Hence, an accurate representation among choices offered must reflect immediate vulnerability starting right away upon delivery continuing well before reaching full toddler phase distinctly noted
